name: Assault
summary: Units with [ASSAULT] weapons can shoot using assault shooting, even after Advancing.
description: Units containing one or more models with an [ASSAULT] weapon can shoot using assault shooting (10.05).

name: Heavy
summary: +1 to hit if the unit is unengaged, was not set up this turn, and no model moved more than 3" this turn.
description: In your Shooting phase, each time an attack is made with a [HEAVY] weapon, add 1 to the hit roll if all of the following apply to the attacking unit: That unit is unengaged; That unit was not set up on the battlefield this turn; No model in that unit has moved more than 3" this turn.

name: Pistol
summary: Identical to [CLOSE-QUARTERS] for all rules purposes.
description: [PISTOL] and [CLOSE-QUARTERS] are identical for all rules purposes. See [CLOSE-QUARTERS]. Designer's Note: [PISTOL] is a pre-existing ability that will be superseded by [CLOSE-QUARTERS] as this edition of Warhammer 40,000 progresses. Both are functionally the same, but the latter is a more suitable term for weapons that function in this way.

name: Rapid Fire
summary: Add X attack dice when the target is within half range.
description: This ability always takes the form [RAPID FIRE X]. Each time you gather attack dice for a [RAPID FIRE] weapon, add X additional attack dice if the target unit was within half range of that weapon in the Select Targets step.

name: Torrent
summary: Automatically hits the target; no hit roll is made.
description: Each time an attack is made with a [TORRENT] weapon, that attack automatically hits the target.

name: Indirect Fire
summary: Can target units not visible to the attacking model; target has benefit of cover; unmodified 1–5 fails.
description: Units containing one or more models with an [INDIRECT FIRE] weapon can shoot using indirect shooting (10.07). While doing so, [INDIRECT FIRE] weapons can target units not visible to the attacking model. Each time an [INDIRECT FIRE] weapon makes an attack: the target has the benefit of cover; hit rolls cannot be re-rolled; and an unmodified hit roll of 1–5 fails, unless your unit Remained Stationary this turn and the target is visible to one or more friendly units, in which case an unmodified hit roll of 1–3 fails instead.

name: Psychic
summary: Ignore any or all modifiers to BS/WS and the hit roll; attacks count as psychic attacks.
description: Each time an attack is made with a [PSYCHIC] weapon, you can ignore any or all modifiers to that attack's BS or WS characteristic and any or all modifiers to the hit roll. Attacks made with [PSYCHIC] weapons are known as psychic attacks (this can be important for the triggering of other rules).

name: Blast
summary: Add 1 extra attack dice per 5 models in the target unit (rounding down).
description: Each time you gather attack dice for a [BLAST] weapon, add one additional attack dice for every five models that were in the target unit in the Select Targets step (rounding down). If this ability takes the form [BLAST X], each time you gather attack dice for such a weapon, add X additional attack dice for every five models that were in the target unit in the Select Targets step (rounding down) instead.

name: Devastating Wounds
summary: A critical wound ends the attack sequence; the target instead suffers mortal wounds equal to the weapon's D (one model max per critical wound).
description: Each time an attack made with a [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] weapon results in a critical wound, the attack sequence for that attack ends and the target unit suffers a number of mortal wounds equal to the D characteristic of that weapon. These are inflicted after resolving any normal damage inflicted by those attacks. Mortal wounds inflicted by [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] weapons can damage a maximum of one model for each critical wound; any remaining mortal wounds inflicted by that attack are lost.

name: Lethal Hits
summary: A critical hit lets you automatically wound the target without making a wound roll.
description: Each time an attack made with a [LETHAL HITS] weapon results in a critical hit, you can choose for that attack to automatically wound the target. Designer's Note: Choosing to automatically wound the target means that no wound roll is made for that attack. You may decide against this, as it means that attack cannot result in a critical wound and so cannot trigger other abilities such as [DEVASTATING WOUNDS].

name: Melta
summary: Add X to the weapon's D characteristic when the target is within half range.
description: This ability always takes the form [MELTA X]. Each time a model makes an attack with a [MELTA] weapon, if the target unit was within half range of that weapon in the Select Targets step, add X to that weapon's D characteristic until those attacks have been resolved.

name: Sustained Hits
summary: A critical hit generates X additional hits on the target.
description: This ability always takes the form [SUSTAINED HITS X]. Each time an attack made with a [SUSTAINED HITS] weapon results in a critical hit, that attack results in a number of additional hits on the target as denoted by X.

name: Twin-linked
summary: You can re-roll the wound roll.
description: Each time an attack is made with a [TWIN-LINKED] weapon, you can re-roll the wound roll.

name: Anti
summary: Against units with keyword X, an unmodified wound roll of Y+ is a critical wound.
description: This ability always takes the form [ANTI-X Y+]. Each time an attack is made with an [ANTI] weapon, if the target unit has the keyword denoted by X, an unmodified wound roll of Y+ is a critical wound.

name: Precision
summary: At the start of the Allocation Order step, the active player can select an allocation group containing a visible CHARACTER model.
description: While resolving attacks made with one or more [PRECISION] weapons, at the start of the Allocation Order step, if the target unit contains one or more CHARACTER models visible to one or more of the attacking models, the active player can select one allocation group that contains one of those visible CHARACTER models. If they do, until those attacks are resolved or until that CHARACTER group is destroyed (whichever happens first), that CHARACTER group is the current allocation group.

name: Ignores Cover
summary: The target cannot have the benefit of cover against this attack, including from rules like Stealth.
description: Each time an attack is made with an [IGNORES COVER] weapon, the target cannot have the benefit of cover against that attack (13.08), including from rules that give a model or unit the benefit of cover (e.g. Stealth).

name: Lance
summary: Add 1 to the wound roll if the attacking model's unit made a charge move this turn.
description: Each time an attack is made with a [LANCE] weapon, if the attacking model's unit made a charge move this turn, add 1 to the wound roll.

name: Cumbersome
summary: Not present in the current Core Rules.
description: [CUMBERSOME] no longer appears in the Core Abilities list (section 24) of the current Core Rules. No datasheet in this app's data currently uses it.

name: Hazardous
summary: After attacking, make one hazard roll per [HAZARDOUS] weapon selected in the Select Weapons step.
description: Each time a unit is selected to shoot or selected to fight, after that unit has resolved all of its attacks, make a number of hazard rolls (06.03) for that unit equal to the number of [HAZARDOUS] weapons you selected in the Select Weapons step.

name: One Shot
summary: Each weapon with this ability can only be selected to make attacks with once per battle.
description: Each weapon with this ability can only be selected to make attacks with once per battle. If a destroyed model is returned to a unit, all of its [ONE SHOT] weapons that have already been selected to make attacks with during the battle cannot be selected again. If a new unit is added to an army, all [ONE SHOT] weapons in that unit can be selected to make attacks with once per battle.

name: Extra Attacks
summary: Models fight with [EXTRA ATTACKS] weapons in addition to any others; must select all [EXTRA ATTACKS] weapons plus one other melee weapon.
description: Each time a unit containing one or more models with an [EXTRA ATTACKS] weapon fights, those models will make attacks with those weapons in addition to any others. In the Select Weapons step (04.01), for each of those models, you must select: all of that model's [EXTRA ATTACKS] weapons; and one of that model's other melee weapons, if possible.

name: Cleave
summary: If targeting a single unit, add X attack dice per 5 models in the target unit (rounding down).
description: This ability always takes the form [CLEAVE X]. Each time you gather attack dice for a [CLEAVE] weapon, if you only selected one target for all of that weapon's attacks, add X additional attack dice for every five models that were in the target unit in the Select Targets step (rounding down).

name: Close-Quarters
summary: Units can shoot into close combat; non-MONSTER/VEHICLE models must choose either [CLOSE-QUARTERS] weapons or other ranged weapons, not both.
description: Units containing one or more models with a [CLOSE-QUARTERS] weapon can shoot using close-quarters shooting (10.06). When using another shooting type, for each model in that unit (excluding MONSTER/VEHICLE models), you can only select one of the following to make attacks with: one or more of its [CLOSE-QUARTERS] weapons; or one or more of its other ranged weapons.

name: Deadly Demise
summary: On a 6 when destroyed, each unit within 6" suffers X mortal wounds.
description: This ability always takes the form Deadly Demise X. Each time a model in this unit is destroyed, after the units embarked within it (if any) have made their emergency disembark moves, roll one D6. On a 6, that model suffers a deadly demise; each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by X (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6").

name: Deep Strike
summary: Can set up anywhere on battlefield more than 8" from all enemy units when making an ingress move.
description: Each time this unit makes an ingress move (20.04), if every model in this unit has this ability, it can be set up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 8" horizontally from all enemy units, even if that is within your opponent's deployment zone.

name: Feel No Pain
summary: Roll D6 each time model would lose a wound; on X+ that wound is not lost.
description: This ability always takes the form Feel No Pain X+. Each time a model with this ability would lose a wound, roll one D6: on an X+, that wound is not lost.

name: Fights First
summary: Unit fights in the Resolve Fights First Combats step of the Fight phase.
description: While every model in a unit has this ability, that unit is a Fights First unit. See the Resolve Fights First Combats step in the Fight phase (12.04).

name: Firing Deck
summary: TRANSPORT can use weapons of up to X embarked models when it shoots each turn.
description: This ability always takes the form Firing Deck X. In your Shooting phase, each time this TRANSPORT is selected to shoot, if one or more units are embarked within it, resolve the following sequence: 1. Select up to X models embarked within this TRANSPORT (excluding models whose units have already been selected to shoot this phase). 2. For each selected model, select one of its ranged weapons (excluding [ONE SHOT] weapons). 3. Until this TRANSPORT has resolved all of its attacks, it has all of those selected weapons in addition to its other weapons. 4. Until the end of the turn, units embarked within this TRANSPORT are not eligible to shoot.

name: Hover
summary: Do not subtract 2" from movement when taking to the skies.
description: Each time this unit takes to the skies (21.03), do not subtract 2" from the maximum distance.

name: Infiltrators
summary: During deployment, can be set up more than 8" from opponent's deployment zone and all enemy units.
description: During deployment, if every model in a unit has this ability, it can be set up anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 8" horizontally from your opponent's deployment zone and all enemy units.

name: Lone Operative
summary: Not visible to enemy models beyond 12"; cannot be targeted by [INDIRECT FIRE] from beyond 12".
description: Unless part of an attached unit, this unit is not visible to enemy models unless they are within 12" of this unit, and it cannot be targeted by [INDIRECT FIRE] weapons unless the attacking model is within 12" of this unit. If this ability takes the form Lone Operative X", unless part of an attached unit, this unit is not visible to enemy models unless they are within X" of this unit, and it cannot be targeted by [INDIRECT FIRE] weapons unless the attacking model is within X" of this unit.

name: Scouts
summary: Can make a scout move before battle or reposition from strategic reserves.
description: This ability always takes the form Scouts X". In the Resolve Pre-battle Abilities step, if every model in a unit has this ability, you can do one of the following: If that unit is in strategic reserves, you can set up that unit anywhere that is wholly within your deployment zone. If that unit is wholly within your deployment zone, it can make a scout move. If that unit is embarked within a DEDICATED TRANSPORT that is wholly within your deployment zone, and if every model embarked within that DEDICATED TRANSPORT has the Scouts ability, that DEDICATED TRANSPORT can make a scout move. Scout Move — Maximum distance: the X" in Scouts X". After moving: your unit must be more than 8" horizontally from all enemy units.

name: Stealth
summary: Ranged attacks targeting this unit grant it the benefit of cover.
description: If every model in a unit has this ability, each time a ranged attack targets that unit, that unit has the benefit of cover against that attack (13.08).

name: Super-Heavy Walker
summary: Can move through models (excluding TITANIC) and terrain sections 4" or less in height.
description: Each time a unit with this ability makes a normal, advance or fall-back move: Models in that unit can move through models (including MONSTER/VEHICLE models, but excluding TITANIC models) and can move horizontally through sections of terrain features that are 4" or less in height. Before moving that unit, you can select for all models in that unit to have the MOBILE keyword until that move ends. If you do, when that move ends, roll one D6: on a 1, that unit is battle-shocked.

name: Armies
summary: Each player commands an army of units they control.
description: Each player commands an army made up of units of models. You control all the models in your army. If a rule refers to the 'controlling player', it means the player who controls the models being affected by that rule.

name: Units and Models
summary: A unit contains one or more models that move and fight together.
description: A unit can contain one or more models that move and fight together as a single group. Most models have a base, which is also part of that model for all rules purposes. When an effect or ability applies to a unit, it applies to every model in that unit.

name: Active and Opposing Player
summary: One player is always the 'active player'; the other is the 'opposing player'.
description: At any given time, one player is the 'active player' and their opponent is the 'opposing player'. While it is a player's turn, that player is the active player — except when a unit is selected to move, shoot, or fight, in which case that unit's controlling player is the active player until that action ends.

name: Friendly and Enemy
summary: Friendly units are in your army; enemy units are in your opponent's.
description: Friendly units and models are those in your army. Enemy units and models are those in your opponent's army. If a rule affects units or models without specifying friendly or enemy, it affects any unit or model regardless of which army they are in.

name: Keywords
summary: Datasheets list faction keywords and other keywords; all appear in FULL CAPITALS AND BOLD.
description: Datasheets have a list of keywords separated into faction keywords and other keywords. Faction keywords are used when deciding which models to include in your army, but otherwise both types function the same way. Keywords appear in full capitals and bold text throughout the rules.

name: Starting Strength
summary: The number of models in the unit when it is added to the army.
description: The number of models a unit contains when it is first added to your army. For attached units, starting strength is the number of models at the start of the first battle round. Used to determine whether a unit is Below Starting Strength or Below Half-strength.

name: Below Half-strength
summary: Fewer than half the starting models remain (or fewer than half the Wounds, for single-model units).
description: A unit with Starting Strength 1 is below half-strength when its remaining wounds are less than half its W characteristic. Any other unit is below half-strength when its remaining model count is less than half its starting strength. Units at, or below, half-strength must make Battle-shock rolls in the Command phase.

name: Measuring Distances
summary: Distances are in inches; measure to/from the closest part of a model's base (or hull for FRAME models).
description: Distances are measured in inches ("). You can measure distances whenever you want. Unless stated otherwise, measure to or from the closest part of a model's base. For FRAME models (those without a base), measure to and from the closest point on the model itself.

name: Dice
summary: All rolls use D6. D3 = D6÷2 rounded up. 2D6 = roll two dice and add. 2+ means 2 or higher.
description: All dice rolls use six-sided dice (D6). Common notations: 2+ or 3+ (a result of 2 or more, etc.); 1–3 or 4–6 (a range of results); 2D6 or 3D6 (roll multiple dice and add the values); D3 (roll one D6 and halve the result, rounding up); D6+1 (roll and add a stated value).

name: Leadership Rolls
summary: Roll 2D6; succeeds if the result equals or exceeds one or more Ld values in the unit.
description: To make a Leadership roll for a unit, roll 2D6. If the result equals or exceeds one or more of the Ld characteristics of models in that unit, the roll succeeds. Otherwise it fails. The rule that instructed you to make the roll describes the effects of success or failure.

name: Battle-shock Rolls
summary: Make a Leadership roll; on failure the unit becomes battle-shocked (OC '–', no stratagems, no actions).
description: To make a Battle-shock roll for a unit, make a Leadership roll for it. If it succeeds, the unit is not Battle-shocked. If it fails, the unit becomes Battle-shocked. While Battle-shocked: the OC of all models is modified to '–'; its controlling player cannot target it with stratagems; it cannot start or complete an Action.

name: Engagement Range
summary: 2" horizontally and 5" vertically from an enemy model; models within this range are Engaged.
description: While a model is within 2" horizontally and 5" vertically of an enemy model, both models are within Engagement Range of each other. Models cannot end a Normal, Advance, or Fall-back move within Engagement Range of enemy models. A unit with no models within Engagement Range of any enemy model is Unengaged.

name: Unit Coherency
summary: All models must stay within 2" (horizontal) and 5" (vertical) of at least one other model, and within 9" (horizontal) and 5" (vertical) of every other model in the unit.
description: A multi-model unit must be set up and end any movement with all models within 2" horizontally and 5" vertically of at least one other model in the unit, and within 9" horizontally and 5" vertically of every other model in the unit.

name: Normal Move
summary: Move each model up to its M characteristic; cannot end within Engagement Range of enemies.
description: Each model in the unit can move up to a distance in inches equal to its M (Move) characteristic. Models cannot end their move within Engagement Range of enemy models. If a model cannot end its move while maintaining Unit Coherency, it cannot make a move at all.

name: Advance
summary: Roll 1D6 and add to M for this move; unit cannot charge or start Actions this turn.
description: Roll 1D6 before moving any models; add the result to the M characteristic of each model in the unit for this move. A unit that Advances cannot declare a Charge or start an Action this turn. It can still use [ASSAULT] weapons to shoot even after Advancing.

name: Fall Back
summary: Move up to M" while passing through Engagement Range; cannot end within it; cannot charge or act this turn.
description: When a unit Falls Back, each model can move up to its M characteristic, passing through Engagement Range of enemy models but not ending within it. A Falling Back unit cannot shoot (normal shooting), declare a Charge, or start an Action this turn. Choose between Ordered Retreat or Desperate Escape mode.

name: Ordered Retreat
summary: Standard fall-back mode; models pass through enemies but cannot end within Engagement Range.
description: The default mode when Falling Back. Models can move through the Engagement Range of enemy models but must not end their move within Engagement Range of any enemy model. Available to any non-Battle-shocked unit.

name: Desperate Escape
summary: Before moving, make a Hazard Roll for each model in the unit. Mandatory for Battle-shocked units Falling Back.
description: Mandatory fall-back mode for a Battle-shocked unit (or any unit, if chosen voluntarily). Before moving, make a Hazard Roll (CO029) for each model in the unit. While moving, each model can be moved through enemy models. After moving, if the unit was not already Battle-shocked, it must make a Battle-shock roll.

name: Flying
summary: FLYING units can Take to the Skies: –2" move, but ignore vertical distance and all model/terrain obstacles.
description: When a FLYING unit is selected to make a Normal, Advance, Fall-back, or Charge move, the active player can declare it Takes to the Skies. If it does: subtract 2" from the maximum distance for that move; the unit ignores all vertical distances when moving; and it can move through all types of model and all categories of terrain feature.

name: Hit Roll
summary: Roll 1D6 per attack; hits on BS/WS or higher. Unmodified 1 always fails; unmodified 6 is a Critical Hit.
description: For each attack, roll 1D6. An unmodified result of 1 always fails. An unmodified result of 6 is a Critical Hit. Any other result hits if it equals or exceeds the weapon's BS (ranged attacks) or WS (melee attacks).

name: Critical Hit
summary: An unmodified roll of 6 on the Hit Roll; triggers abilities like [LETHAL HITS] and [SUSTAINED HITS].
description: An unmodified Hit Roll result of 6. Critical Hits are still hits and also trigger special weapon abilities such as [LETHAL HITS] (auto-wound) and [SUSTAINED HITS] (extra hits). Some abilities specify a different critical hit threshold.

name: Wound Roll
summary: Roll 1D6 per hit; result needed based on S vs T. Unmodified 1 always fails; unmodified 6 is a Critical Wound.
description: For each hit, roll 1D6. An unmodified 1 always fails. An unmodified 6 is a Critical Wound. Other results wound based on the weapon's Strength vs the target's Toughness: S ≥ 2×T → 2+; S > T → 3+; S = T → 4+; S < T → 5+; S ≤ T/2 → 6+.

name: Critical Wound
summary: An unmodified roll of 6 on the Wound Roll; triggers abilities like [DEVASTATING WOUNDS].
description: An unmodified Wound Roll result of 6 (or the threshold set by [ANTI]). Critical Wounds are still wounds and also trigger special weapon abilities such as [DEVASTATING WOUNDS]. They cannot be avoided by [LETHAL HITS] auto-wounds.

name: Strength vs Toughness
summary: Required wound roll: S ≥ 2×T = 2+, S > T = 3+, S = T = 4+, S < T = 5+, S ≤ T/2 = 6+.
description: When making a Wound Roll, the result needed depends on how the weapon's Strength (S) compares to the target's Toughness (T): if S is twice T or more → 2+; if S is greater than T → 3+; if S equals T → 4+; if S is less than T → 5+; if S is half T or less → 6+.

name: Save Roll
summary: Opposing player rolls 1D6 per wound; modified by AP. Unmodified 1 always fails.
description: After attacks are allocated, the opposing player makes one Save Roll per wound. Modify the result by the weapon's AP characteristic (negative AP worsens the roll). If the modified result equals or exceeds the model's Sv characteristic, the save succeeds and no damage is inflicted. An unmodified roll of 1 always fails. The opposing player may instead use their InSv (if any), which is never modified by AP.

name: Invulnerable Save
summary: A save characteristic (InSv) that is never modified by AP; choose it instead of the normal Sv.
description: A save characteristic that is never modified by Armour Penetration. Each time a model with an InSv makes a Save Roll, it can choose to use its InSv or its Sv — but not both. An invulnerable save of 1+ does not exist; the minimum effective value is 2+.

name: Damage Allocation
summary: Divide the target unit into allocation groups; resolve attacks against the current group in order.
description: The opposing player divides the target unit into allocation groups: one per CHARACTER model, plus one group per set of non-CHARACTER models sharing the same W, Sv, and InSv characteristics. Groups with wounded non-CHARACTER models go first. CHARACTER groups are always last. Attacks are resolved against the current group from lowest to highest dice result.

name: Mortal Wounds
summary: Direct damage bypassing hit, wound, and save rolls; each inflicts 1 damage regardless of the weapon's D.
description: Mortal wounds bypass all normal attack rolls and save rolls. Each mortal wound inflicts 1 point of damage on the target unit. Excess mortal wounds after destroying a model carry over to the next model in the unit. Rules such as [DEVASTATING WOUNDS] inflict mortal wounds equal to the weapon's D characteristic.

name: Hazard Roll
summary: Roll 1D6: on 1–2 the unit suffers 1 mortal wound (3 if all models are MONSTER/VEHICLE).
description: To make a Hazard Roll for a unit, roll one D6. On a 1–2, that roll fails and the unit suffers 1 mortal wound, or 3 mortal wounds if every model in that unit is a MONSTER or VEHICLE model. If more than one Hazard Roll is required, make all of them simultaneously before resolving any failures.

name: Plunging Fire
summary: +1 BS when the attacker is on terrain 3"+ high, or has TOWERING and the target is within 12".
description: Each time a model makes a ranged attack targeting a visible unit with models on ground level, improve the attacker's BS by 1 if: the attacking model is on a terrain section 3" or more in height; OR the attacking model has the TOWERING keyword and the target unit is within 12".

name: Visible
summary: A model is visible if a straight, unobstructed line (1mm wide) can be drawn to any part of it.
description: A model is visible to another if you can draw a straight, unobstructed line — at least 1mm wide — between any part of one model and any part of the other. Terrain features and other models between them may block this line of sight.

name: Fully Visible
summary: Every part of the model facing the observer is visible; only the model's own body can block sight to itself.
description: A model is fully visible if every part of it that faces the observing model is visible. Only the model's own body (not its base, weapon barrels, etc.) can block visibility to itself. Weapon and equipment extensions are ignored.

name: Unit Visible and Fully Visible
summary: 'Unit visible' = at least one model visible; 'Unit fully visible' = every model fully visible.
description: A unit is visible if one or more of its models are visible to the observing model. A unit is fully visible if every model in it is fully visible to the observing model. These distinctions matter for the Benefit of Cover and certain abilities.

name: Benefit of Cover
summary: Worsen the attacker's BS by 1; granted when models are within terrain or not fully visible through it.
description: A unit has the Benefit of Cover against a ranged attack if all its models are INFANTRY, BEASTS, or SWARM models wholly within a terrain feature's area; or the unit is not fully visible to the attacking model due to intervening terrain. Benefit of cover worsens the attacker's BS by 1. Cannot be gained against [IGNORES COVER] weapons.

name: Exposed Terrain
summary: Provides minimal protection and no movement restriction; all models move freely through it.
description: Terrain with no special movement restrictions and minimal protective value. All models can traverse it without penalty. Exposed terrain does not grant the Benefit of Cover. Examples: craters, razorwire, scattered debris.

name: Light Terrain
summary: Can provide cover; does not restrict movement — all models, including MONSTERS/VEHICLES, move through it freely.
description: Light terrain can provide the Benefit of Cover to units within it, but will not slow an enemy's advance: all models, including MONSTER/VEHICLE models, can move horizontally and vertically through light terrain features without restriction. Light terrain does not block line of sight on its own.

name: Dense Terrain
summary: Blocks line of sight and may carry Hidden/Obscuring/Solid; INFANTRY/BEASTS/SWARM/MOBILE pass through horizontally, others only through sections 2" or less in height.
description: Dense terrain blocks line of sight and may have additional rules: Hidden, Obscuring, and/or Solid. INFANTRY, BEASTS, and SWARM models can move both horizontally and vertically through dense terrain features. MOBILE models can also move through them horizontally. Other models can only move horizontally through a dense terrain feature where every section they pass through is 2" or less in height (otherwise they must move vertically over it or go around); they cannot move through dense terrain vertically at all.

name: Hidden
summary: INFANTRY/BEASTS/SWARM in dense terrain are only visible to enemies within 15" if they haven't recently shot.
description: A unit is Hidden if all its models are INFANTRY, BEASTS, or SWARM models wholly within a dense terrain feature, and the unit did not shoot this turn or the previous turn. Hidden models are only visible to enemy models within 15".

name: Obscuring
summary: Dense terrain that blocks all lines of sight between models on opposite sides of it.
description: Dense terrain with the Obscuring rule blocks every line of sight drawn between models on opposite sides of the terrain, even if neither model is within the terrain feature.

name: Solid
summary: Dense terrain where enclosed gaps 3" or less above ground block line of sight through walls.
description: Dense terrain with the Solid rule: lines of sight cannot pass through enclosed gaps that are 3" or less above ground level. This represents solid walls of buildings and similar enclosed structures.

name: Objective Control (OC)
summary: A model characteristic for contesting objectives; '–' means the model cannot contest objectives.
description: A model's OC (Objective Control) characteristic determines how effectively it contests an objective marker. A model with OC '–' cannot contest objectives. While a unit is Battle-shocked, the OC of all its models is modified to '–'.

name: Level of Control
summary: Sum of OC of your models within range of an objective; highest level controls it (ties = neither).
description: A player's level of control over an objective marker equals the total OC of their models within range. The player with the higher level controls the objective. If tied, neither controls it — unless one has Secured it.

name: Secured Objective
summary: An objective held by a rule; remains under your control even without nearby units, until outcontrolled.
description: When a rule says an objective is Secured by your army, it remains under your control even if you have no models nearby, until your opponent achieves a higher level of control over it.

name: Command Points (CP)
summary: Currency for using Stratagems; both players gain 1 CP at the start of each Command Phase.
description: Command Points are spent to use Stratagems. Both players gain 1 CP at the start of each Command Phase (Gain Core CP step). Some rules grant additional CP or increase your CP limit.

name: Stratagems
summary: Tactical abilities that cost CP; each specifies WHEN, TARGET, EFFECT, and RESTRICTIONS.
description: Stratagems are tactical abilities that cost Command Points. Each stratagem states: WHEN (the timing window), TARGET (which units), EFFECT (what happens), and any RESTRICTIONS. You cannot use the same Stratagem more than once per phase, or target the same unit with more than one Stratagem per phase.

name: Actions
summary: Battlefield tasks units perform to score mission objectives; unit cannot shoot or charge while doing one.
description: Actions are listed in missions and other rules. Each Action states: WHEN it starts; which UNITS can do it; USE LIMIT; COMPLETES condition; and EFFECT. A unit cannot start an Action if it is not on the battlefield, is AIRCRAFT or a FORTIFICATION, is Battle-shocked, has OC 0 or '–', is Engaged (unless TITANIC), Advanced or Fell Back this turn, or already started another Action this turn.

name: Transport Capacity
summary: Max number and type of friendly models that can embark; shown on the TRANSPORT's datasheet.
description: TRANSPORT models have a transport capacity on their datasheet, specifying the type and maximum number of friendly models that can embark. Units can start the battle already embarked — declare this during the Muster Armies step.

name: Embarking
summary: After a move, unit can embark if all models end within 3" of the TRANSPORT.
description: A friendly unit can embark within a friendly TRANSPORT after making a Normal, Advance, or Fall-back move, if: all models end within 3" of the TRANSPORT; the unit was not set up this turn; the unit is eligible per the TRANSPORT's datasheet; and the TRANSPORT has sufficient capacity. Remove the unit from the battlefield.

name: Disembarking
summary: Embarked units disembark in the Movement Phase; choose Rapid, Tactical, or Combat Disembark mode.
description: In the Movement phase, units embarked in a TRANSPORT can disembark. Modes: Rapid (after TRANSPORT's Normal/Ingress move; unit cannot Charge); Tactical (TRANSPORT Remains Stationary; unit can then make a Normal or Advance move); Combat (any other case; Hazard Roll per model; unit Battle-shocked; cannot Charge).

name: Emergency Disembark
summary: When a TRANSPORT is destroyed, embarked units set up within 6", make Hazard Rolls, and are battle-shocked.
description: When a TRANSPORT is destroyed, all embarked units immediately Emergency Disembark: set up within 6" of the TRANSPORT's position, not within Engagement Range of enemies. Make a Hazard Roll for each model. Each unit is Battle-shocked and cannot Charge until end of turn. Models that cannot be set up are destroyed.

name: Monsters and Vehicles Movement
summary: MONSTER/VEHICLE models can move through friendly and enemy models (not other MONSTER/VEHICLE).
description: During Normal and Advance moves, MONSTER and VEHICLE models can be moved through the spaces occupied by friendly and enemy models. They cannot move through other MONSTER or VEHICLE models. All other movement rules still apply.

name: Frame
summary: Keyword for models without a base; measure distances to/from the model's closest point.
description: Models with the FRAME keyword do not have a standard base. Whenever a rule requires measuring distances to or from a FRAME model, measure to and from the closest point on the model. When rotating during a move, a FRAME model turns around its central axis while remaining upright.

name: Shooting at Engaged Monsters/Vehicles
summary: Engaged MONSTER/VEHICLE units can be targeted; –1 to Hit (except [CLOSE-QUARTERS] from Engaged attackers).
description: Enemy MONSTER or VEHICLE units that are Engaged with friendly units can still be selected as targets of your ranged attacks. Subtract 1 from the Hit Roll for each such attack, except for [CLOSE-QUARTERS] attacks made by models that are themselves Engaged with the target.

name: Attached Units
summary: A Leader joins a Bodyguard unit; CHARACTER models cannot be targeted while Bodyguard models remain.
description: Before the battle, Leader and Support units can be attached to eligible Bodyguard units, forming a single Attached Unit. While Bodyguard models remain, attacks cannot be allocated to CHARACTER models in that unit. When all Bodyguard models are destroyed, the CHARACTER unit splits off and becomes independent. Each Bodyguard unit can have one Leader and one Support attached.

name: Strategic Reserves
summary: Units held off the board; arrive via Ingress moves from Round 2; total cannot exceed 50% of points.
description: Before the battle, you can place friendly units in Strategic Reserves instead of deploying them. The combined points of Strategic Reserves units cannot exceed 50% of your total army points. They arrive via Ingress moves from Round 2 onwards. At the end of Round 3, unreturned Strategic Reserves units are destroyed.

name: Ingress Move
summary: Set up within 6" of a battlefield edge, more than 8" from all enemies; cannot move again until Charge Phase.
description: When a Strategic Reserves unit arrives, set up all its models wholly within 6" of one or more battlefield edges and more than 8" horizontally from all enemy units. Before Round 3, models cannot be placed in the opponent's deployment zone. After arriving, the unit cannot make any other type of move until the start of the next Charge phase.

name: Aircraft
summary: Must deploy in Strategic Reserves; only make Ingress moves; return to reserves at end of opponent's turn.
description: AIRCRAFT units must be placed in Strategic Reserves during deployment. They can only make Ingress moves. At the end of your opponent's turn, all your AIRCRAFT on the battlefield return to Strategic Reserves. AIRCRAFT cannot declare Charges and can only make melee attacks against FLYING units. Only FLYING units can declare Charges against AIRCRAFT.

name: Surge Move
summary: Move up to X" toward a target; must end Engaged if possible; cannot move again this phase.
description: When a Surge Move is triggered, the unit moves each model up to the stated distance toward a designated target. Each model must end Engaged with the target if possible. If not possible, it must end as close to the target as possible. No model can end within Engagement Range of units other than the Surge target.

name: Aura Abilities
summary: Affect models/units within a stated range; a model is always within range of its own aura.
description: Abilities tagged 'Aura' affect models or units within a stated range. The model with the aura ability is always within range of its own aura. A unit can be affected by multiple different aura abilities simultaneously, but each specific aura can only affect a given unit once per trigger.

name: Faction Abilities
summary: Abilities common to units sharing a faction keyword; only apply when your army faction matches.
description: Faction abilities are common to units sharing a particular faction keyword. Unless stated otherwise, a unit's faction abilities only apply if the army faction you selected while mustering your army matches a faction keyword on that unit's datasheet.

name: Psychic Abilities
summary: Tagged 'Psychic'; wounds inflicted by them count as psychic attacks for other rules.
description: Abilities tagged with 'Psychic' are psychic abilities. If a psychic ability causes a model to lose wounds, each of those wounds is inflicted by a psychic attack. This distinction can trigger other rules that specifically reference psychic attacks.

name: Wargear Abilities
summary: Granted by specific wargear items; apply to the whole unit or bearer model only.
description: Wargear abilities are granted when a unit or model has a specific item of wargear. If the ability affects the whole unit, it applies to all models. If it applies to the model carrying it, that model is the 'bearer' and the ability applies until the bearer is destroyed.
